data.alert_description: Investigate network reachability failure: source 192.168.0.12 cannot reach destination 172.16.0.12 (25 pings sent, 0 successful). Goal: determine cause and restore connectivity.

facts.claim: router1 DHCP pool 'R1_LAN' -> network 192.168.0.0/24, default-router 192.168.0.1, dns 8.8.8.8, domain 'lab-local'
router1 OSPF neighbor 3.3.3.3 via Serial0/1/1 (neighbor address 10.0.0.9)
router3 OSPF neighbor 1.1.1.1 via Serial0/1/0 (neighbor address 10.0.0.10)
router3 OSPF neighbor 2.2.2.2 via Serial0/1/1 (neighbor address 10.0.0.5)
router1 OSPF: GigabitEthernet0/0/0 interface state DR
router2: config/OSPF/DHCP fetch failed (All connection attempts failed)

sources.name: get_ospf:router2
sources.content: { "error": "Interfaces fetch failed: All connection attempts failed" }

sources.name: get_ospf:router3
sources.content: { "ospf_full": [ ... Loopback1, Serial0/1/1 neighbor neighbor_address: 10.0.0.5, Serial0/1/0 neighbor neighbor_address: 10.0.0.10 ... ] }

root_causes.cause: Both R1 and R3 have static/ip-route-interface-forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24 that point at the other router, creating a forwarding loop so ICMP from 192.168.0.12 never reaches the actual 172.16.0.12.
evidence: R1 running-config/routing: "prefix: 172.16.0.0, mask: 255.255.255.0, fwd-list: [{ fwd: 10.0.0.9 }]" (R1 forwards 172.16/24 to 10.0.0.9)
R3 running-config/routing: "prefix: 172.16.0.0, mask: 255.255.255.0, fwd-list: [{ fwd: 10.0.0.10 }]" (R3 forwards 172.16/24 to 10.0.0.10)

root_causes.cause: Router2 is not reachable by the management tools and R1 shows no OSPF neighbor on its Serial0/1/0 interface (the R1<->R2 link), so the real path to the 172.16.0.0/24 network via R2 (if that is the correct destination path) is not available.
evidence: get_running_config for router2 returned: "{ "error": "All connection attempts failed" }" (router2 management unreachable)
R1 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 entry shows interface_state Point-to-Point with "neighbors": [] (no OSPF neighbor on R1<->R2)
Topology metadata: noted physical/config links to router2 from router1 and router3, but remote side not reachable; router3 does see R2 (R3 OSPF shows neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.5) while R1 does not

missing_info: Authoritative location of 172.16.0.12 (which device/interface/network hosts this IP?)

Router2 operational state and configuration (running-config, interface IPs on the two serial links, and OSPF neighbor/status) — router2 is currently unreachable by the tooling and is needed to determine whether R2 should be the next hop for 172.16.0.0/24

Router2 operational state and configuration (running-config, interface IPs on the two serial links, and OSPF neighbor/status) — router2 is currently unreachable by the tooling and is needed to determine whether R2 should be the next hop for 172.16.0.0/24

data.intent_description: Both R1 and R3 have static routes for 172.16.0.0/24 that forward to each other, creating a forwarding loop that prevents 192.168.0.12 from reaching 172.16.0.12. Restore end-to-end reachability by removing the incorrect static next-hops so routing can converge via the correct path (and allow OSPF/other routes to be installed).

plan.problem: Forwarding loop for 172.16.0.0/24 due to static routes on R1 and R3 pointing at each other; ICMP from 192.168.0.12 cannot reach 172.16.0.12.
plan.fix_summary: Remove the incorrect static route for 172.16.0.0/24 on R1 and R3 that point at each other. Operational risk: brief route loss for any traffic relying on those static entries; OSPF or alternate routes may be installed afterward. Do not add replacement routes until authoritative reachability to router2/172.16.0.12 is confirmed.

R1 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 entry shows interface_state Point-to-Point with "neighbors": [] (no OSPF neighbor on R1<->R2)
Topology metadata: noted physical/config links to router2 from router1 and router3, but remote side not reachable; router3 does see R2 (R3 OSPF shows neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.5) while R1 does not

missing_info: Authoritative location of 172.16.0.12 (which device/interface/network hosts this IP?)

Router2 operational state and configuration (running-config, interface IPs on the two serial links, and OSPF neighbor/status) — router2 is currently unreachable by the tooling and is needed to determine whether R2 should be the next hop for 172.16.0.0/24

plan.problem: Forwarding loop for 172.16.0.0/24 due to static routes on R1 and R3 pointing at each other; ICMP from 192.168.0.12 cannot reach 172.16.0.12.
plan.fix_summary: Remove the incorrect static route for 172.16.0.0/24 on R1 and R3 that point at each other. Operational risk: brief route loss for any traffic relying on those static entries; OSPF or alternate routes may be installed afterward. Do not add replacement routes until authoritative reachability to router2/172.16.0.12 is confirmed.

evidence.detail: router1 OSPF neighbor 3.3.3.3 @10.0.0.9 observed on Serial0/1/1
router3 OSPF neighbor 1.1.1.1 @10.0.0.10 observed on Serial0/1/0
interface Serial0/1/0 description 'Link to R2', ip 10.0.0.1/30
interface Serial0/1/1 description 'Link to R2', ip 10.0.0.6/30

evidence: get_running_config:router1 (call_7Pv7RVNF8LzxG0AmJoWUNdFs) returned "routing": null — no ip-route-interface-forwarding entry for 172.16.0.0/24 present in R1's running-config.

get_running_config:router3 (call_cb0b3fJwRc2GTWGveJTZhQIM) shows routing.ip-route-interface-forwarding-list only contains prefix 0.0.0.0 -> Loopback1 — no ip-route-interface-forwarding entry for 172.16.0.0/24 on R3.

get_ospf:router1 (call_Zv2ZWTDpit63oWU2bRsDITBW) and get_ospf:router3 (call_aWcZSMGsBzPmtIqipfhNtUM1) show OSPF adjacency between R1 and R3 on their interconnect, and R1's Serial0/1/0 has no OSPF neighbor — confirms the static forwarding entries that created the loop are not present on R1/R3 but R2 remains unreachable.
remaining_issues: Authoritative location/owner of 172.16.0.12 is not confirmed — cannot prove end-to-end reachability from 192.168.0.12 to that host.

Router2 is still unreachable by the management tooling (connection attempts failed); its interfaces, routing, and OSPF state are unknown and may still be required to restore full path to 172.16.0.0/24 if R2 is the true next hop.
No end-to-end connectivity test (ping/traceroute) from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 was performed as part of verification.

data.intent_description: Both R1 and R3 have static routes for 172.16.0.0/24 that forward to each other, creating a forwarding loop that prevents 192.168.0.12 from reaching 172.16.0.12. Restore end-to-end reachability by removing the incorrect static next-hops so routing can converge via the correct path (and allow OSPF/other routes to be installed).

root_causes.cause: Both R1 and R3 have static/ip-route-interface-forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24 that point at the other router, creating a forwarding loop so ICMP from 192.168.0.12 never reaches the actual 172.16.0.12.
evidence: R1 running-config/routing: "prefix: 172.16.0.0, mask: 255.255.255.0, fwd-list: [{ fwd: 10.0.0.9 }]" (R1 forwards 172.16/24 to 10.0.0.9)
R3 running-config/routing: "prefix: 172.16.0.0, mask: 255.255.255.0, fwd-list: [{ fwd: 10.0.0.10 }]" (R3 forwards 172.16/24 to 10.0.0.10)

root_causes.cause: Router2 is not reachable by the management tools and R1 shows no OSPF neighbor on its Serial0/1/0 interface (the R1<->R2 link), so the real path to the 172.16.0.0/24 network via R2 (if that is the correct destination path) is not available.

R1 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 entry shows interface_state Point-to-Point with "neighbors": [] (no OSPF neighbor on R1<->R2)
Topology metadata: noted physical/config links to router2 from router1 and router3, but remote side not reachable; router3 does see R2 (R3 OSPF shows neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.5) while R1 does not

messages.content:
What was observed
- Traffic from 192.168.0.12 could not reach 172.16.0.12 because R1 and R3 each had a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 that pointed at the other router, creating a forwarding loop.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Misconfigured static/ip-route-interface-forwarding entries on R1 and R3 (each forwarding 172.16.0.0/24 to the other). Router2 is also unreachable by management, so the correct next-hop for 172.16.0.0/24 could not be confirmed.

Changes made
- Removed the static route for 172.16.0.0/24 from R1.
- Removed the static route for 172.16.0.0/24 from R3.

Verification
- Passed: R1 and R3 running-configs no longer contain ip-route-interface-forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24; OSPF adjacency between R1 and R3 remains present.
- Remaining issues: Router2 is still unreachable (management/tools) and its config/OSPF state are unknown; the authoritative location of 172.16.0.12 was not confirmed; no end-to-end ping/traceroute from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 has been performed.

Recommended next steps
- Restore management/connectivity to Router2, confirm where 172.16.0.12 is attached, then run an end-to-end ping/traceroute to verify full reachability.

data.last_message:
What was observed
- Traffic from 192.168.0.12 could not reach 172.16.0.12 because R1 and R3 each had a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 that pointed at the other router, creating a forwarding loop.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Misconfigured static/ip-route-interface-forwarding entries on R1 and R3 (each forwarding 172.16.0.0/24 to the other). Router2 is also unreachable by management, so the correct next-hop for 172.16.0.0/24 could not be confirmed.

- Removed the static route for 172.16.0.0/24 from R3.

Verification
- Passed: R1 and R3 running-configs no longer contain ip-route-interface-forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24; OSPF adjacency between R1 and R3 remains present.
- Remaining issues: Router2 is still unreachable (management/tools) and its config/OSPF state are unknown; the authoritative location of 172.16.0.12 was not confirmed; no end-to-end ping/traceroute from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 has been performed.

Recommended next steps
- Restore management/connectivity to Router2, confirm where 172.16.0.12 is attached, then run an end-to-end ping/traceroute to verify full reachability.
